OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO INSPECTION DEMAND; DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT MONASTERIO, ERICA BISGYER

DISCOVERY 610, NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DEMAND FOR INSPECTION -- THE COURT HAS JURISDICTION TO CONSIDER PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL AN INSPECTION BASED ON THE SECOND DEMAND FOR INSPECTION SERVED ON OCTOBER 25, 2004. THE MOTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ESTABLISHED GOOD CAUSE FOR THE INSPECTION. ADDITIONALLY, THE COURT FINDS THAT THE DISCOVERY SOUGHT IS OBTAINABLE FROM A LESS BURDENSOME AND MORE CONVENIENT SOURCE. DEFENDANT HAS PROVIDED THE SPECIFICATIONS, APPROVED BUILDING PLANS, AND PERMITS FOR THE WHEELCHAIR LIFT THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF THE INSPECTION DEMAND. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN
NOTICE OF UNAVAILABILITY OF ZALOB, DAVID STEPHEN ON JAN-22-2005 THROUGH JAN-30-2005 FILED BY PLAINTIFF ARIS, SHARON GURNEY, ANDREW
